tools.name: authorization.getAuthorize
tools.description:
Authorize a user by sending them through the [Box](https://box.com)
website and request their permission to act on their behalf.

This is the first step when authenticating a user using
OAuth 2.0. To request a user's authorization to use the Box APIs
on their behalf you will need to send a user to the URL with this
format.

tools.name: authorization.postOauth2Revoke
tools.description:
Revoke an active Access Token, effectively logging a user out
that has been previously authenticated.

tools.name: authorization.postOauth2Token
tools.description:
Request an Access Token using either a client-side obtained OAuth 2.0
authorization code or a server-side JWT assertion.

An Access Token is a string that enables Box to verify that a
request belongs to an authorized session. In the normal order of
operations you will begin by requesting authentication from the
[authorize](#get-authorize) endpoint and Box will send you an
authorization code.

You will then send this code to this endpoint to exchange it for
an Access Token. The returned Access Token can then be used to to make
Box API calls.

tools.name: authorization.postOauth2TokenRefresh
tools.description: Refresh an Access Token using its client ID, secret, and refresh token.

tools.name: boxSign.getSignRequests
tools.description:
Gets sign requests created by a user. If the `sign_files` and/or
`parent_folder` are deleted, the sign request will not return in the list.

tools.name: boxSign.getSignRequestsId
tools.description: Gets a sign request by ID.

tools.name: boxSign.postSignRequests
tools.description:
Creates a sign request. This involves preparing a document for signing and
sending the sign request to signers.

tools.name: boxSign.postSignRequestsIdCancel
tools.description: Cancels a sign request.

tools.name: boxSign.postSignRequestsIdResend
tools.description: Resends a sign request email to all outstanding signers.

tools.name: classifications.deleteMetadataTemplatesEnterpriseSecurityClassification6VmVochwUWoSchema
tools.description:
Delete all classifications by deleting the classification
metadata template.

tools.name: classifications.getMetadataTemplatesEnterpriseSecurityClassification6VmVochwUWoSchema
tools.description:
Retrieves the classification metadata template and lists all the
classifications available to this enterprise.

This API can also be called by including the enterprise ID in the
URL explicitly, for example
`/metadata_templates/enterprise_12345/securityClassification-6VMVochwUWo/schema`.

tools.name: classifications.postMetadataTemplatesSchemaClassifications
tools.description:
When an enterprise does not yet have any classifications, this API call
initializes the classification template with an initial set of
classifications.

If an enterprise already has a classification, the template will already
exist and instead an API call should be made to add additional
classifications.

tools.name: classifications.putMetadataTemplatesEnterpriseSecurityClassification6VmVochwUWoSchemaAdd
tools.description:
Adds one or more new classifications to the list of classifications
available to the enterprise.

This API can also be called by including the enterprise ID in the
URL explicitly, for example
`/metadata_templates/enterprise_12345/securityClassification-6VMVochwUWo/schema`.

tools.name: classifications.putMetadataTemplatesEnterpriseSecurityClassification6VmVochwUWoSchemaDelete
tools.description:
Removes a classification from the list of classifications
available to the enterprise.

This API can also be called by including the enterprise ID in the
URL explicitly, for example
`/metadata_templates/enterprise_12345/securityClassification-6VMVochwUWo/schema`.

tools.name: classifications.putMetadataTemplatesEnterpriseSecurityClassification6VmVochwUWoSchemaUpdate
tools.description:
Updates the labels and descriptions of one or more classifications
available to the enterprise.

This API can also be called by including the enterprise ID in the
URL explicitly, for example
`/metadata_templates/enterprise_12345/securityClassification-6VMVochwUWo/schema`.

tools.name: classificationsOnFiles.deleteFilesIdMetadataEnterpriseSecurityClassification6VmVochwUWo
tools.description:
Removes any classifications from a file.

This API can also be called by including the enterprise ID in the
URL explicitly, for example
`/files/:id//enterprise_12345/securityClassification-6VMVochwUWo`.

tools.name: classificationsOnFiles.getFilesIdMetadataEnterpriseSecurityClassification6VmVochwUWo
tools.description:
Retrieves the classification metadata instance that
has been applied to a file.

This API can also be called by including the enterprise ID in the
URL explicitly, for example
`/files/:id//enterprise_12345/securityClassification-6VMVochwUWo`.

tools.name: classificationsOnFiles.postFilesIdMetadataEnterpriseSecurityClassification6VmVochwUWo
tools.description:
Adds a classification to a file by specifying the label of the
classification to add.

This API can also be called by including the enterprise ID in the
URL explicitly, for example
`/files/:id//enterprise_12345/securityClassification-6VMVochwUWo`.

tools.name: classificationsOnFiles.putFilesIdMetadataEnterpriseSecurityClassification6VmVochwUWo
tools.description:
Updates a classification on a file.

The classification can only be updated if a classification has already been
applied to the file before. When editing classifications, only values are
defined for the enterprise will be accepted.

tools.name: classificationsOnFolders.deleteFoldersIdMetadataEnterpriseSecurityClassification6VmVochwUWo
tools.description:
Removes any classifications from a folder.

This API can also be called by including the enterprise ID in the
URL explicitly, for example
`/folders/:id//enterprise_12345/securityClassification-6VMVochwUWo`.

tools.name: classificationsOnFolders.getFoldersIdMetadataEnterpriseSecurityClassification6VmVochwUWo
tools.description:
Retrieves the classification metadata instance that
has been applied to a folder.

This API can also be called by including the enterprise ID in the
URL explicitly, for example
`/folders/:id//enterprise_12345/securityClassification-6VMVochwUWo`.

tools.name: classificationsOnFolders.postFoldersIdMetadataEnterpriseSecurityClassification6VmVochwUWo
tools.description:
Adds a classification to a folder by specifying the label of the
classification to add.

This API can also be called by including the enterprise ID in the
URL explicitly, for example
`/folders/:id//enterprise_12345/securityClassification-6VMVochwUWo`.

tools.name: classificationsOnFolders.putFoldersIdMetadataEnterpriseSecurityClassification6VmVochwUWo
tools.description:
Updates a classification on a folder.

The classification can only be updated if a classification has already been
applied to the folder before. When editing classifications, only values are
defined for the enterprise will be accepted.

tools.name: collaborations.deleteCollaborationsId
tools.description: Deletes a single collaboration.

tools.name: collaborations.getCollaborationsId
tools.description: Retrieves a single collaboration.

tools.name: collaborations.postCollaborations
tools.description:
Adds a collaboration for a single user or a single group to a file
or folder.

Collaborations can be created using email address, user IDs, or a
group IDs.

If a collaboration is being created with a group, access to
this endpoint is dependent on the group's ability to be invited.

tools.name: collaborations.putCollaborationsId
tools.description:
Updates a collaboration.
Can be used to change the owner of an item, or to
accept collaboration invites.

tools.name: collaborationsList.getCollaborations
tools.description: Retrieves all pending collaboration invites for this user.

tools.name: collaborationsList.getFilesIdCollaborations
tools.description:
Retrieves a list of pending and active collaborations for a
file. This returns all the users that have access to the file
or have been invited to the file.

tools.name: collaborationsList.getFoldersIdCollaborations
tools.description:
Retrieves a list of pending and active collaborations for a
folder. This returns all the users that have access to the folder
or have been invited to the folder.

tools.name: collaborationsList.getGroupsIdCollaborations
tools.description:
Retrieves all the collaborations for a group. The user
must have admin permissions to inspect enterprise's groups.

Each collaboration object has details on which files or
folders the group has access to and with what role.

tools.name: collections.getCollections
tools.description:
Retrieves all collections for a given user.

Currently, only the `favorites` collection
is supported.

tools.name: collections.getCollectionsIdItems
tools.description:
Retrieves the files and/or folders contained within
this collection.

tools.name: comments.deleteCommentsId
tools.description: Permanently deletes a comment.

tools.name: comments.getCommentsId
tools.description:
Retrieves the message and metadata for a specific comment, as well
as information on the user who created the comment.

tools.name: comments.getFilesIdComments
tools.description: Retrieves a list of comments for a file.

tools.name: comments.postComments
tools.description:
Adds a comment by the user to a specific file, or
as a reply to an other comment.

tools.name: comments.putCommentsId
tools.description: Update the message of a comment.

tools.name: devicePinners.deleteDevicePinnersId
tools.description: Deletes an individual device pin.

tools.name: devicePinners.getDevicePinnersId
tools.description: Retrieves information about an individual device pin.

tools.name: devicePinners.getEnterprisesIdDevicePinners
tools.description:
Retrieves all the device pins within an enterprise.

The user must have admin privileges, and the application
needs the "manage enterprise" scope to make this call.

tools.name: domainRestrictionsForCollaborations.deleteCollaborationWhitelistEntriesId
tools.description:
Removes a domain from the list of domains that have been deemed safe to create
collaborations for within the current enterprise.

tools.name: domainRestrictionsForCollaborations.getCollaborationWhitelistEntries
tools.description:
Returns the list domains that have been deemed safe to create collaborations
for within the current enterprise.

tools.name: domainRestrictionsForCollaborations.getCollaborationWhitelistEntriesId
tools.description:
Returns a domain that has been deemed safe to create collaborations
for within the current enterprise.

tools.name: domainRestrictionsForCollaborations.postCollaborationWhitelistEntries
tools.description:
Creates a new entry in the list of allowed domains to allow
collaboration for.

tools.name: domainRestrictionsUserExemptions.deleteCollaborationWhitelistExemptTargetsId
tools.description:
Removes a user's exemption from the restrictions set out by the allowed list
of domains for collaborations.

tools.name: domainRestrictionsUserExemptions.getCollaborationWhitelistExemptTargets
tools.description:
Returns a list of users who have been exempt from the collaboration
domain restrictions.

tools.name: domainRestrictionsUserExemptions.getCollaborationWhitelistExemptTargetsId
tools.description:
Returns a users who has been exempt from the collaboration
domain restrictions.

tools.name: domainRestrictionsUserExemptions.postCollaborationWhitelistExemptTargets
tools.description:
Exempts a user from the restrictions set out by the allowed list of domains
for collaborations.

tools.name: downloads.getFilesIdContent
tools.description: Returns the contents of a file in binary format.

tools.name: emailAliases.deleteUsersIdEmailAliasesId
tools.description: Removes an email alias from a user.

tools.name: emailAliases.getUsersIdEmailAliases
tools.description:
Retrieves all email aliases for a user. The collection
does not include the primary login for the user.

tools.name: emailAliases.postUsersIdEmailAliases
tools.description: Adds a new email alias to a user account..

tools.name: events.getEvents
tools.description:
Returns up to a year of past events for a given user
or for the entire enterprise.

By default this returns events for the authenticated user. To retrieve events
for the entire enterprise, set the `stream_type` to `admin_logs_streaming`
for live monitoring of new events, or `admin_logs` for querying across
historical events. The user making the API call will
need to have admin privileges, and the application will need to have the
scope `manage enterprise properties` checked.

tools.name: events.optionsEvents
tools.description:
Returns a list of real-time servers that can be used for long-polling updates
to the [event stream](#get-events).

Long polling is the concept where a HTTP request is kept open until the
server sends a response, then repeating the process over and over to receive
updated responses.

Long polling the event stream can only be used for user events, not for
enterprise events.

To use long polling, first use this endpoint to retrieve a list of long poll
URLs. Next, make a long poll request to any of the provided URLs.

When an event occurs in monitored account a response with the value
`new_change` will be sent. The response contains no other details as
it only serves as a prompt to take further action such as sending a
request to the [events endpoint](#get-events) with the last known
`stream_position`.

After the server sends this response it closes the connection. You must now
repeat the long poll process to begin listening for events again.

If no events occur for a while and the connection times out you will
receive a response with the value `reconnect`. When you receive this response
you’ll make another call to this endpoint to restart the process.

If you receive no events in `retry_timeout` seconds then you will need to
make another request to the real-time server (one of the URLs in the response
for this endpoint). This might be necessary due to network errors.

Finally, if you receive a `max_retries` error when making a request to the
real-time server, you should start over by making a call to this endpoint
first.

tools.name: fileRequests.deleteFileRequestsId
tools.description: Deletes a file request permanently.

tools.name: fileRequests.getFileRequestsId
tools.description: Retrieves the information about a file request.

tools.name: fileRequests.postFileRequestsIdCopy
tools.description:
Copies an existing file request that is already present on one folder,
and applies it to another folder.

tools.name: fileRequests.putFileRequestsId
tools.description:
Updates a file request. This can be used to activate or
deactivate a file request.

tools.name: files.deleteFilesId
tools.description:
Deletes a file, either permanently or by moving it to
the trash.

The the enterprise settings determine whether the item will
be permanently deleted from Box or moved to the trash.

tools.name: files.getFilesId
tools.description: Retrieves the details about a file.

tools.name: files.getFilesIdThumbnailId
tools.description:
Retrieves a thumbnail, or smaller image representation, of a file.

Sizes of `32x32`,`64x64`, `128x128`, and `256x256` can be returned in
the `.png` format and sizes of `32x32`, `160x160`, and `320x320`
can be returned in the `.jpg` format.

Thumbnails can be generated for the image and video file formats listed
[found on our community site][1].

[1]: https://community.box.com/t5/Migrating-and-Previewing-Content/File-Types-and-Fonts-Supported-in-Box-Content-Preview/ta-p/327

tools.name: files.optionsFilesContent
tools.description:
Performs a check to verify that a file will be accepted by Box
before you upload the entire file.

tools.name: files.postFilesIdCopy
tools.description: Creates a copy of a file.

tools.name: files.putFilesId
tools.description:
Updates a file. This can be used to rename or move a file,
create a shared link, or lock a file.

tools.name: fileVersionLegalHolds.getFileVersionLegalHolds
tools.description:
Get a list of file versions on legal hold for a legal hold
assignment.

Due to ongoing re-architecture efforts this API might not return all file
versions for this policy ID.

Instead, this API will only return file versions held in the legacy
architecture. Two new endpoints will available to request any file versions
held in the new architecture.

For file versions held in the new architecture, the `GET
/legal_hold_policy_assignments/:id/file_versions_on_hold` API can be used to
return all past file versions available for this policy assignment, and the
`GET /legal_hold_policy_assignments/:id/files_on_hold` API can be used to
return any current (latest) versions of a file under legal hold.

The `GET /legal_hold_policy_assignments?policy_id={id}` API can be used to
find a list of policy assignments for a given policy ID.

Once the re-architecture is completed this API will be deprecated.

tools.name: fileVersionLegalHolds.getFileVersionLegalHoldsId
tools.description:
Retrieves information about the legal hold policies
assigned to a file version.

tools.name: fileVersionRetentions.getFileVersionRetentions
tools.description: Retrieves all file version retentions for the given enterprise.

tools.name: fileVersionRetentions.getFileVersionRetentionsId
tools.description: Returns information about a file version retention.

tools.name: fileVersions.deleteFilesIdVersionsId
tools.description:
Move a file version to the trash.

Versions are only tracked for Box users with premium accounts.

tools.name: fileVersions.getFilesIdVersions
tools.description:
Retrieve a list of the past versions for a file.

Versions are only tracked by Box users with premium accounts. To fetch the ID
of the current version of a file, use the `GET /file/:id` API.

tools.name: fileVersions.getFilesIdVersionsId
tools.description:
Retrieve a specific version of a file.

Versions are only tracked for Box users with premium accounts.

tools.name: fileVersions.postFilesIdVersionsCurrent
tools.description:
Promote a specific version of a file.

If previous versions exist, this method can be used to
promote one of the older versions to the top of the version history.

This creates a new copy of the old version and puts it at the
top of the versions history. The file will have the exact same contents
as the older version, with the the same hash digest, `etag`, and
name as the original.

Other properties such as comments do not get updated to their
former values.

Don't use this endpoint to restore Box Notes,
as it works with file formats such as PDF, DOC,
PPTX or similar.

tools.name: fileVersions.putFilesIdVersionsId
tools.description:
Restores a specific version of a file after it was deleted.
Don't use this endpoint to restore Box Notes,
as it works with file formats such as PDF, DOC,
PPTX or similar.

tools.name: folderLocks.deleteFolderLocksId
tools.description:
Deletes a folder lock on a given folder.

You must be authenticated as the owner or co-owner of the folder to
use this endpoint.

tools.name: folderLocks.getFolderLocks
tools.description:
Retrieves folder lock details for a given folder.

You must be authenticated as the owner or co-owner of the folder to
use this endpoint.

tools.name: folderLocks.postFolderLocks
tools.description:
Creates a folder lock on a folder, preventing it from being moved and/or
deleted.

You must be authenticated as the owner or co-owner of the folder to
use this endpoint.

tools.name: folders.deleteFoldersId
tools.description:
Deletes a folder, either permanently or by moving it to
the trash.

tools.name: folders.getFoldersId
tools.description:
Retrieves details for a folder, including the first 100 entries
in the folder.

To fetch more items within the folder, please use the
[Get items in a folder](#get-folders-id-items) endpoint.

tools.name: folders.getFoldersIdItems
tools.description:
Retrieves a page of items in a folder. These items can be files,
folders, and web links.

To request more information about the folder itself, like its size,
please use the [Get a folder](#get-folders-id) endpoint instead.

tools.name: folders.postFolders
tools.description: Creates a new empty folder within the specified parent folder.

tools.name: folders.postFoldersIdCopy
tools.description:
Creates a copy of a folder within a destination folder.

The original folder will not be changed.

tools.name: folders.putFoldersId
tools.description:
Updates a folder. This can be also be used to move the folder,
create shared links, update collaborations, and more.

tools.name: groupMemberships.deleteGroupMembershipsId
tools.description:
Deletes a specific group membership. Only admins of this
group or users with admin-level permissions will be able to
use this API.

tools.name: groupMemberships.getGroupMembershipsId
tools.description:
Retrieves a specific group membership. Only admins of this
group or users with admin-level permissions will be able to
use this API.

tools.name: groupMemberships.getGroupsIdMemberships
tools.description:
Retrieves all the members for a group. Only members of this
group or users with admin-level permissions will be able to
use this API.

tools.name: groupMemberships.getUsersIdMemberships
tools.description:
Retrieves all the groups for a user. Only members of this
group or users with admin-level permissions will be able to
use this API.

tools.name: groupMemberships.postGroupMemberships
tools.description:
Creates a group membership. Only users with
admin-level permissions will be able to use this API.

tools.name: groupMemberships.putGroupMembershipsId
tools.description:
Updates a user's group membership. Only admins of this
group or users with admin-level permissions will be able to
use this API.

tools.name: groups.deleteGroupsId
tools.description:
Permanently deletes a group. Only users with
admin-level permissions will be able to use this API.

tools.name: groups.getGroups
tools.description:
Retrieves all of the groups for a given enterprise. The user
must have admin permissions to inspect enterprise's groups.

tools.name: groups.getGroupsId
tools.description:
Retrieves information about a group. Only members of this
group or users with admin-level permissions will be able to
use this API.

tools.name: groups.postGroups
tools.description:
Creates a new group of users in an enterprise. Only users with admin
permissions can create new groups.

tools.name: groups.postGroupsTerminateSessions
tools.description:
Validates the roles and permissions of the group,
and creates asynchronous jobs
to terminate the group's sessions.
Returns the status for the POST request.

tools.name: groups.putGroupsId
tools.description:
Updates a specific group. Only admins of this
group or users with admin-level permissions will be able to
use this API.

tools.name: invites.getInvitesId
tools.description: Returns the status of a user invite.

tools.name: invites.postInvites
tools.description:
Invites an existing external user to join an enterprise.

The existing user can not be part of another enterprise and
must already have a Box account. Once invited, the user will receive an
email and are prompted to accept the invitation within the
Box web application.

This method requires the "Manage An Enterprise" scope enabled for
the application, which can be enabled within the developer console.

tools.name: legalHoldPolicies.deleteLegalHoldPoliciesId
tools.description:
Delete an existing legal hold policy.

This is an asynchronous process. The policy will not be
fully deleted yet when the response returns.

tools.name: legalHoldPolicies.getLegalHoldPolicies
tools.description:
Retrieves a list of legal hold policies that belong to
an enterprise.

tools.name: legalHoldPolicies.getLegalHoldPoliciesId
tools.description: Retrieve a legal hold policy.

tools.name: legalHoldPolicies.postLegalHoldPolicies
tools.description: Create a new legal hold policy.

tools.name: legalHoldPolicies.putLegalHoldPoliciesId
tools.description: Update legal hold policy.

tools.name: legalHoldPolicyAssignments.deleteLegalHoldPolicyAssignmentsId
tools.description:
Remove a legal hold from an item.

This is an asynchronous process. The policy will not be
fully removed yet when the response returns.

tools.name: legalHoldPolicyAssignments.getLegalHoldPolicyAssignments
tools.description: Retrieves a list of items a legal hold policy has been assigned to.

tools.name: legalHoldPolicyAssignments.getLegalHoldPolicyAssignmentsId
tools.description: Retrieve a legal hold policy assignment.

tools.name: legalHoldPolicyAssignments.getLegalHoldPolicyAssignmentsIdFilesOnHold
tools.description:
Get a list of current file versions for a legal hold
assignment.

In some cases you may want to get previous file versions instead. In these
cases, use the `GET  /legal_hold_policy_assignments/:id/file_versions_on_hold`
API instead to return any previous versions of a file for this legal hold
policy assignment.

Due to ongoing re-architecture efforts this API might not return all file
versions held for this policy ID. Instead, this API will only return the
latest file version held in the newly developed architecture. The `GET
/file_version_legal_holds` API can be used to fetch current and past versions
of files held within the legacy architecture.

The `GET /legal_hold_policy_assignments?policy_id={id}` API can be used to
find a list of policy assignments for a given policy ID.

tools.name: legalHoldPolicyAssignments.getLegalHoldPolicyAssignmentsIdFileVersionsOnHold
tools.description:
Get a list of previous file versions for a legal hold
assignment.

In some cases you may only need the latest file versions instead. In these
cases, use the `GET  /legal_hold_policy_assignments/:id/files_on_hold` API
instead to return any current (latest) versions of a file for this legal hold
policy assignment.

Due to ongoing re-architecture efforts this API might not return all files
held for this policy ID. Instead, this API will only return past file versions
held in the newly developed architecture. The `GET /file_version_legal_holds`
API can be used to fetch current and past versions of files held within the
legacy architecture.

The `GET /legal_hold_policy_assignments?policy_id={id}` API can be used to
find a list of policy assignments for a given policy ID.

tools.name: legalHoldPolicyAssignments.postLegalHoldPolicyAssignments
tools.description: Assign a legal hold to a file, file version, folder, or user.

tools.name: metadataCascadePolicies.deleteMetadataCascadePoliciesId
tools.description: Deletes a metadata cascade policy.

tools.name: metadataCascadePolicies.getMetadataCascadePolicies
tools.description:
Retrieves a list of all the metadata cascade policies
that are applied to a given folder. This can not be used on the root
folder with ID `0`.

tools.name: metadataCascadePolicies.getMetadataCascadePoliciesId
tools.description: Retrieve a specific metadata cascade policy assigned to a folder.

tools.name: metadataCascadePolicies.postMetadataCascadePolicies
tools.description:
Creates a new metadata cascade policy that applies a given
metadata template to a given folder and automatically
cascades it down to any files within that folder.

In order for the policy to be applied a metadata instance must first
be applied to the folder the policy is to be applied to.

tools.name: metadataCascadePolicies.postMetadataCascadePoliciesIdApply
tools.description:
Force the metadata on a folder with a metadata cascade policy to be applied to
all of its children. This can be used after creating a new cascade policy to
enforce the metadata to be cascaded down to all existing files within that
folder.

tools.name: metadataInstancesFiles.deleteFilesIdMetadataIdId
tools.description: Deletes a piece of file metadata.

tools.name: metadataInstancesFiles.getFilesIdMetadata
tools.description: Retrieves all metadata for a given file.

tools.name: metadataInstancesFiles.getFilesIdMetadataIdId
tools.description:
Retrieves the instance of a metadata template that has been applied to a
file.

tools.name: metadataInstancesFiles.postFilesIdMetadataIdId
tools.description:
Applies an instance of a metadata template to a file.

In most cases only values that are present in the metadata template
will be accepted, except for the `global.properties` template which accepts
any key-value pair.

tools.name: metadataInstancesFiles.putFilesIdMetadataIdId
tools.description:
Updates a piece of metadata on a file.

The metadata instance can only be updated if the template has already been
applied to the file before. When editing metadata, only values that match
the metadata template schema will be accepted.

The update is applied atomically. If any errors occur during the
application of the operations, the metadata instance will not be changed.

tools.name: metadataInstancesFolders.deleteFoldersIdMetadataIdId
tools.description: Deletes a piece of folder metadata.

tools.name: metadataInstancesFolders.getFoldersIdMetadata
tools.description:
Retrieves all metadata for a given folder. This can not be used on the root
folder with ID `0`.

tools.name: metadataInstancesFolders.getFoldersIdMetadataIdId
tools.description:
Retrieves the instance of a metadata template that has been applied to a
folder. This can not be used on the root folder with ID `0`.

tools.name: metadataInstancesFolders.postFoldersIdMetadataIdId
tools.description:
Applies an instance of a metadata template to a folder.

In most cases only values that are present in the metadata template
will be accepted, except for the `global.properties` template which accepts
any key-value pair.

To display the metadata template in the Box web app the enterprise needs to be
configured to enable **Cascading Folder Level Metadata** for the user in the
admin console.

tools.name: metadataInstancesFolders.putFoldersIdMetadataIdId
tools.description:
Updates a piece of metadata on a folder.

The metadata instance can only be updated if the template has already been
applied to the folder before. When editing metadata, only values that match
the metadata template schema will be accepted.

The update is applied atomically. If any errors occur during the
application of the operations, the metadata instance will not be changed.

tools.name: metadataTemplates.deleteMetadataTemplatesIdIdSchema
tools.description:
Delete a metadata template and its instances.
This deletion is permanent and can not be reversed.

tools.name: metadataTemplates.getMetadataTemplates
tools.description:
Finds a metadata template by searching for the ID of an instance of the
template.

tools.name: metadataTemplates.getMetadataTemplatesEnterprise
tools.description:
Used to retrieve all metadata templates created to be used specifically within
the user's enterprise

tools.name: metadataTemplates.getMetadataTemplatesGlobal
tools.description:
Used to retrieve all generic, global metadata templates available to all
enterprises using Box.

tools.name: metadataTemplates.getMetadataTemplatesId
tools.description: Retrieves a metadata template by its ID.

tools.name: metadataTemplates.getMetadataTemplatesIdIdSchema
tools.description:
Retrieves a metadata template by its `scope` and `templateKey` values.

To find the `scope` and `templateKey` for a template, list all templates for
an enterprise or globally, or list all templates applied to a file or folder.

tools.name: metadataTemplates.postMetadataTemplatesSchema
tools.description:
Creates a new metadata template that can be applied to
files and folders.

tools.name: metadataTemplates.putMetadataTemplatesIdIdSchema
tools.description:
Updates a metadata template.

The metadata template can only be updated if the template
already exists.

The update is applied atomically. If any errors occur during the
application of the operations, the metadata template will not be changed.

tools.name: recentItems.getRecentItems
tools.description:
Returns information about the recent items accessed
by a user, either in the last 90 days or up to the last
1000 items accessed.

tools.name: retentionPolicies.deleteRetentionPoliciesId
tools.description: Permanently deletes a retention policy.

tools.name: retentionPolicies.getRetentionPolicies
tools.description: Retrieves all of the retention policies for an enterprise.

tools.name: retentionPolicies.getRetentionPoliciesId
tools.description: Retrieves a retention policy.

tools.name: retentionPolicies.postRetentionPolicies
tools.description: Creates a retention policy.

tools.name: retentionPolicies.putRetentionPoliciesId
tools.description: Updates a retention policy.

tools.name: retentionPolicyAssignments.deleteRetentionPolicyAssignmentsId
tools.description:
Removes a retention policy assignment
applied to content.

tools.name: retentionPolicyAssignments.getRetentionPoliciesIdAssignments
tools.description:
Returns a list of all retention policy assignments associated with a specified
retention policy.

tools.name: retentionPolicyAssignments.getRetentionPolicyAssignmentsId
tools.description: Retrieves a retention policy assignment

tools.name: retentionPolicyAssignments.getRetentionPolicyAssignmentsIdFilesUnderRetention
tools.description: Returns a list of files under retention for a retention policy assignment.

tools.name: retentionPolicyAssignments.getRetentionPolicyAssignmentsIdFileVersionsUnderRetention
tools.description:
Returns a list of file versions under retention for a retention policy
assignment.

tools.name: retentionPolicyAssignments.postRetentionPolicyAssignments
tools.description: Assigns a retention policy to an item.

tools.name: search.getMetadataQueryIndices
tools.description: Retrieves the metadata query indices for a given scope and template key.

tools.name: search.getSearch
tools.description:
Searches for files, folders, web links, and shared files across the
users content or across the entire enterprise.

tools.name: search.postMetadataQueriesExecuteRead
tools.description:
Create a search using SQL-like syntax to return items that match specific
metadata.

By default, this endpoint returns only the most basic info about the items for
which the query matches. To get additional fields for each item, including any
of the metadata, use the `fields` attribute in the query.

tools.name: sharedLinksFiles.getFilesIdGetSharedLink
tools.description: Gets the information for a shared link on a file.

tools.name: sharedLinksFiles.getSharedItems
tools.description:
Returns the file represented by a shared link.

A shared file can be represented by a shared link,
which can originate within the current enterprise or within another.

This endpoint allows an application to retrieve information about a
shared file when only given a shared link.

The `shared_link_permission_options` array field can be returned
by requesting it in the `fields` query parameter.

tools.name: sharedLinksFiles.putFilesIdAddSharedLink
tools.description: Adds a shared link to a file.

tools.name: sharedLinksFiles.putFilesIdRemoveSharedLink
tools.description: Removes a shared link from a file.

tools.name: sharedLinksFiles.putFilesIdUpdateSharedLink
tools.description: Updates a shared link on a file.

tools.name: sharedLinksFolders.getFoldersIdGetSharedLink
tools.description: Gets the information for a shared link on a folder.

tools.name: sharedLinksFolders.getSharedItemsFolders
tools.description:
Return the folder represented by a shared link.

A shared folder can be represented by a shared link,
which can originate within the current enterprise or within another.

This endpoint allows an application to retrieve information about a
shared folder when only given a shared link.

tools.name: sharedLinksFolders.putFoldersIdAddSharedLink
tools.description: Adds a shared link to a folder.

tools.name: sharedLinksFolders.putFoldersIdRemoveSharedLink
tools.description: Removes a shared link from a folder.

tools.name: sharedLinksFolders.putFoldersIdUpdateSharedLink
tools.description: Updates a shared link on a folder.

tools.name: sharedLinksWebLinks.getSharedItemsWebLinks
tools.description:
Returns the web link represented by a shared link.

A shared web link can be represented by a shared link,
which can originate within the current enterprise or within another.

This endpoint allows an application to retrieve information about a
shared web link when only given a shared link.

tools.name: sharedLinksWebLinks.getWebLinksIdGetSharedLink
tools.description: Gets the information for a shared link on a web link.

tools.name: sharedLinksWebLinks.putWebLinksIdAddSharedLink
tools.description: Adds a shared link to a web link.

tools.name: sharedLinksWebLinks.putWebLinksIdRemoveSharedLink
tools.description: Removes a shared link from a web link.

tools.name: sharedLinksWebLinks.putWebLinksIdUpdateSharedLink
tools.description: Updates a shared link on a web link.

tools.name: shieldInformationBarrierReports.getShieldInformationBarrierReports
tools.description: Lists shield information barrier reports with specific IDs.

tools.name: shieldInformationBarrierReports.getShieldInformationBarrierReportsId
tools.description: Retrieves a shield information barrier report by its ID.

tools.name: shieldInformationBarrierReports.postShieldInformationBarrierReports
tools.description: Creates a shield information barrier report for a given barrier.

tools.name: shieldInformationBarriers.getShieldInformationBarriers
tools.description:
Retrieves a list of shield information barrier objects
for the enterprise of JWT.

tools.name: shieldInformationBarriers.getShieldInformationBarriersId
tools.description: Get shield information barrier based on provided ID..

tools.name: shieldInformationBarriers.postShieldInformationBarriers
tools.description:
Creates a shield information barrier to
separate individuals/groups within the same
firm and prevents confidential information passing between them.

tools.name: shieldInformationBarriers.postShieldInformationBarriersChangeStatus
tools.description: Change status of shield information barrier with the specified ID.

tools.name: shieldInformationBarrierSegmentMembers.deleteShieldInformationBarrierSegmentMembersId
tools.description:
Deletes a shield information barrier
segment member based on provided ID.

tools.name: shieldInformationBarrierSegmentMembers.getShieldInformationBarrierSegmentMembers
tools.description:
Lists shield information barrier segment members
based on provided segment IDs.

tools.name: shieldInformationBarrierSegmentMembers.getShieldInformationBarrierSegmentMembersId
tools.description:
Retrieves a shield information barrier
segment member by its ID.

tools.name: shieldInformationBarrierSegmentMembers.postShieldInformationBarrierSegmentMembers
tools.description: Creates a new shield information barrier segment member.

tools.name: shieldInformationBarrierSegmentRestrictions.deleteShieldInformationBarrierSegmentRestrictionsId
tools.description:
Delete shield information barrier segment restriction
based on provided ID.

tools.name: shieldInformationBarrierSegmentRestrictions.getShieldInformationBarrierSegmentRestrictions
tools.description:
Lists shield information barrier segment restrictions
based on provided segment ID.

tools.name: shieldInformationBarrierSegmentRestrictions.getShieldInformationBarrierSegmentRestrictionsId
tools.description:
Retrieves a shield information barrier segment
restriction based on provided ID.

tools.name: shieldInformationBarrierSegmentRestrictions.postShieldInformationBarrierSegmentRestrictions
tools.description:
Creates a shield information barrier
segment restriction object.

tools.name: shieldInformationBarrierSegments.deleteShieldInformationBarrierSegmentsId
tools.description:
Deletes the shield information barrier segment
based on provided ID.

tools.name: shieldInformationBarrierSegments.getShieldInformationBarrierSegments
tools.description:
Retrieves a list of shield information barrier segment objects
for the specified Information Barrier ID.

tools.name: shieldInformationBarrierSegments.getShieldInformationBarrierSegmentsId
tools.description: Retrieves shield information barrier segment based on provided ID..

tools.name: shieldInformationBarrierSegments.postShieldInformationBarrierSegments
tools.description: Creates a shield information barrier segment.

tools.name: shieldInformationBarrierSegments.putShieldInformationBarrierSegmentsId
tools.description: Updates the shield information barrier segment based on provided ID..

tools.name: skills.deleteFilesIdMetadataGlobalBoxSkillsCards
tools.description: Removes any Box Skills cards metadata from a file.

tools.name: skills.getFilesIdMetadataGlobalBoxSkillsCards
tools.description: List the Box Skills metadata cards that are attached to a file.

tools.name: skills.postFilesIdMetadataGlobalBoxSkillsCards
tools.description: Applies one or more Box Skills metadata cards to a file.

tools.name: skills.putFilesIdMetadataGlobalBoxSkillsCards
tools.description: Updates one or more Box Skills metadata cards to a file.

tools.name: skills.putSkillInvocationsId
tools.description:
An alternative method that can be used to overwrite and update all Box Skill
metadata cards on a file.

tools.name: storagePolicies.getStoragePolicies
tools.description: Fetches all the storage policies in the enterprise.

tools.name: storagePolicies.getStoragePoliciesId
tools.description: Fetches a specific storage policy.

tools.name: storagePolicyAssignments.deleteStoragePolicyAssignmentsId
tools.description:
Delete a storage policy assignment.

Deleting a storage policy assignment on a user
will have the user inherit the enterprise's default
storage policy.

There is a rate limit for calling this endpoint of only
twice per user in a 24 hour time frame.

tools.name: storagePolicyAssignments.getStoragePolicyAssignments
tools.description: Fetches all the storage policy assignment for an enterprise or user.

tools.name: storagePolicyAssignments.getStoragePolicyAssignmentsId
tools.description: Fetches a specific storage policy assignment.

tools.name: storagePolicyAssignments.postStoragePolicyAssignments
tools.description: Creates a storage policy assignment for an enterprise or user.

tools.name: storagePolicyAssignments.putStoragePolicyAssignmentsId
tools.description: Updates a specific storage policy assignment.

tools.name: taskAssignments.deleteTaskAssignmentsId
tools.description: Deletes a specific task assignment.

tools.name: taskAssignments.getTaskAssignmentsId
tools.description: Retrieves information about a task assignment.

tools.name: taskAssignments.getTasksIdAssignments
tools.description: Lists all of the assignments for a given task.

tools.name: taskAssignments.postTaskAssignments
tools.description:
Assigns a task to a user.

A task can be assigned to more than one user by creating multiple
assignments.

tools.name: taskAssignments.putTaskAssignmentsId
tools.description:
Updates a task assignment. This endpoint can be
used to update the state of a task assigned to a user.

tools.name: tasks.deleteTasksId
tools.description: Removes a task from a file.

tools.name: tasks.getFilesIdTasks
tools.description:
Retrieves a list of all the tasks for a file. This
endpoint does not support pagination.

tools.name: tasks.getTasksId
tools.description: Retrieves information about a specific task.

tools.name: tasks.postTasks
tools.description:
Creates a single task on a file. This task is not assigned to any user and
will need to be assigned separately.

tools.name: tasks.putTasksId
tools.description:
Updates a task. This can be used to update a task's configuration, or to
update its completion state.

tools.name: termsOfService.getTermsOfServices
tools.description:
Returns the current terms of service text and settings
for the enterprise.

tools.name: termsOfService.getTermsOfServicesId
tools.description: Fetches a specific terms of service.

tools.name: termsOfService.postTermsOfServices
tools.description:
Creates a terms of service for a given enterprise
and type of user.

tools.name: termsOfService.putTermsOfServicesId
tools.description: Updates a specific terms of service.

tools.name: termsOfServiceUserStatuses.getTermsOfServiceUserStatuses
tools.description:
Retrieves an overview of users and their status for a
terms of service, including Whether they have accepted
the terms and when.

tools.name: termsOfServiceUserStatuses.postTermsOfServiceUserStatuses
tools.description: Sets the status for a terms of service for a user.

tools.name: termsOfServiceUserStatuses.putTermsOfServiceUserStatusesId
tools.description: Updates the status for a terms of service for a user.

tools.name: transferFolders.putUsersIdFolders0
tools.description:
Move all of the items (files, folders and workflows) owned by a user into
another user's account

Only the root folder (`0`) can be transferred.

Folders can only be moved across users by users with administrative
permissions.

All existing shared links and folder-level collaborations are transferred
during the operation. Please note that while collaborations at the individual
file-level are transferred during the operation, the collaborations are
deleted when the original user is deleted.

This call will be performed synchronously which might lead to a slow response
when the source user has a large number of items in all of its folders.

If the destination path has a metadata cascade policy attached to any of
the parent folders, a metadata cascade operation will be kicked off
asynchronously.

There is currently no way to check for when this operation is finished.

The destination folder's name will be in the format `{User}'s Files and
Folders`, where `{User}` is the display name of the user.

To make this API call your application will need to have the "Read and write
all files and folders stored in Box" scope enabled.

Please make sure the destination user has access to `Relay` or `Relay Lite`,
and has access to the files and folders involved in the workflows being
transferred.

Admins will receive an email when the operation is completed.

tools.name: trashedFiles.deleteFilesIdTrash
tools.description:
Permanently deletes a file that is in the trash.
This action cannot be undone.

tools.name: trashedFiles.getFilesIdTrash
tools.description:
Retrieves a file that has been moved to the trash.

Please note that only if the file itself has been moved to the
trash can it be retrieved with this API call. If instead one of
its parent folders was moved to the trash, only that folder
can be inspected using the
[`GET /folders/:id/trash`](e://get_folders_id_trash) API.

To list all items that have been moved to the trash, please
use the [`GET /folders/trash/items`](e://get-folders-trash-items/)
API.

tools.name: trashedFiles.postFilesId
tools.description:
Restores a file that has been moved to the trash.

An optional new parent ID can be provided to restore the file to in case the
original folder has been deleted.

tools.name: trashedFolders.deleteFoldersIdTrash
tools.description:
Permanently deletes a folder that is in the trash.
This action cannot be undone.

tools.name: trashedFolders.getFoldersIdTrash
tools.description:
Retrieves a folder that has been moved to the trash.

Please note that only if the folder itself has been moved to the
trash can it be retrieved with this API call. If instead one of
its parent folders was moved to the trash, only that folder
can be inspected using the
[`GET /folders/:id/trash`](e://get_folders_id_trash) API.

To list all items that have been moved to the trash, please
use the [`GET /folders/trash/items`](e://get-folders-trash-items/)
API.

tools.name: trashedFolders.postFoldersId
tools.description:
Restores a folder that has been moved to the trash.

An optional new parent ID can be provided to restore the folder to in case the
original folder has been deleted.

# Folder locking

During this operation, part of the file tree will be locked, mainly
the source folder and all of its descendants, as well as the destination
folder.

For the duration of the operation, no other move, copy, delete, or restore
operation can performed on any of the locked folders.

tools.name: trashedItems.getFoldersTrashItems
tools.description:
Retrieves the files and folders that have been moved
to the trash.

Any attribute in the full files or folders objects can be passed
in with the `fields` parameter to retrieve those specific
attributes that are not returned by default.

This endpoint defaults to use offset-based pagination, yet also supports
marker-based pagination using the `marker` parameter.

tools.name: trashedWebLinks.deleteWebLinksIdTrash
tools.description:
Permanently deletes a web link that is in the trash.
This action cannot be undone.

tools.name: trashedWebLinks.getWebLinksIdTrash
tools.description: Retrieves a web link that has been moved to the trash.

tools.name: trashedWebLinks.postWebLinksId
tools.description:
Restores a web link that has been moved to the trash.

An optional new parent ID can be provided to restore the  web link to in case
the original folder has been deleted.

tools.name: uploads.postFilesContent
tools.description:
Uploads a small file to Box. For file sizes over 50MB we recommend
using the Chunk Upload APIs.

# Request body order

The `attributes` part of the body must come **before** the
`file` part. Requests that do not follow this format when
uploading the file will receive a HTTP `400` error with a
`metadata_after_file_contents` error code.

tools.name: uploads.postFilesIdContent
tools.description:
Update a file's content. For file sizes over 50MB we recommend
using the Chunk Upload APIs.

# Request body order

The `attributes` part of the body must come **before** the
`file` part. Requests that do not follow this format when
uploading the file will receive a HTTP `400` error with a
`metadata_after_file_contents` error code.

tools.name: uploadsChunked.deleteFilesUploadSessionsId
tools.description:
Abort an upload session and discard all data uploaded.

This cannot be reversed.

tools.name: uploadsChunked.getFilesUploadSessionsId
tools.description: Return information about an upload session.

tools.name: uploadsChunked.getFilesUploadSessionsIdParts
tools.description:
Return a list of the chunks uploaded to the upload
session so far.

tools.name: uploadsChunked.postFilesIdUploadSessions
tools.description: Creates an upload session for an existing file.

tools.name: uploadsChunked.postFilesUploadSessions
tools.description: Creates an upload session for a new file.

tools.name: uploadsChunked.postFilesUploadSessionsIdCommit
tools.description:
Close an upload session and create a file from the
uploaded chunks.

tools.name: uploadsChunked.putFilesUploadSessionsId
tools.description: Updates a chunk of an upload session for a file.

tools.name: userAvatars.deleteUsersIdAvatar
tools.description:
Removes an existing user avatar.
You cannot reverse this operation.

tools.name: userAvatars.getUsersIdAvatar
tools.description: Retrieves an image of a the user's avatar.

tools.name: userAvatars.postUsersIdAvatar
tools.description: Adds or updates a user avatar.

tools.name: users.deleteUsersId
tools.description:
Deletes a user. By default this will fail if the user
still owns any content. Move their owned content first
before proceeding, or use the `force` field to delete
the user and their files.

tools.name: users.getUsers
tools.description:
Returns a list of all users for the Enterprise along with their `user_id`,
`public_name`, and `login`.

The application and the authenticated user need to
have the permission to look up users in the entire
enterprise.

tools.name: users.getUsersId
tools.description:
Retrieves information about a user in the enterprise.

The application and the authenticated user need to
have the permission to look up users in the entire
enterprise.

This endpoint also returns a limited set of information
for external users who are collaborated on content
owned by the enterprise for authenticated users with the
right scopes. In this case, disallowed fields will return
null instead.

tools.name: users.getUsersMe
tools.description:
Retrieves information about the user who is currently authenticated.

In the case of a client-side authenticated OAuth 2.0 application
this will be the user who authorized the app.

In the case of a JWT, server-side authenticated application
this will be the service account that belongs to the application
by default.

Use the `As-User` header to change who this API call is made on behalf of.

tools.name: users.postUsers
tools.description:
Creates a new managed user in an enterprise. This endpoint
is only available to users and applications with the right
admin permissions.

tools.name: users.postUsersTerminateSessions
tools.description:
Validates the roles and permissions of the user,
and creates asynchronous jobs
to terminate the user's sessions.
Returns the status for the POST request.

tools.name: users.putUsersId
tools.description:
Updates a managed or app user in an enterprise. This endpoint
is only available to users and applications with the right
admin permissions.

tools.name: watermarksFiles.deleteFilesIdWatermark
tools.description: Removes the watermark from a file.

tools.name: watermarksFiles.getFilesIdWatermark
tools.description: Retrieve the watermark for a file.

tools.name: watermarksFiles.putFilesIdWatermark
tools.description: Applies or update a watermark on a file.

tools.name: watermarksFolders.deleteFoldersIdWatermark
tools.description: Removes the watermark from a folder.

tools.name: watermarksFolders.getFoldersIdWatermark
tools.description: Retrieve the watermark for a folder.

tools.name: watermarksFolders.putFoldersIdWatermark
tools.description: Applies or update a watermark on a folder.

tools.name: webhooks.deleteWebhooksId
tools.description: Deletes a webhook.

tools.name: webhooks.getWebhooks
tools.description:
Returns all defined webhooks for the requesting application.

This API only returns webhooks that are applied to files or folders that are
owned by the authenticated user. This means that an admin can not see webhooks
created by a service account unless the admin has access to those folders, and
vice versa.

tools.name: webhooks.getWebhooksId
tools.description: Retrieves a specific webhook

tools.name: webhooks.postWebhooks
tools.description: Creates a webhook.

tools.name: webhooks.putWebhooksId
tools.description: Updates a webhook.

tools.name: webLinks.deleteWebLinksId
tools.description: Deletes a web link.

tools.name: webLinks.getWebLinksId
tools.description: Retrieve information about a web link.

tools.name: webLinks.postWebLinks
tools.description: Creates a web link object within a folder.

tools.name: webLinks.putWebLinksId
tools.description: Updates a web link object.

tools.name: workflows.getWorkflows
tools.description:
Returns list of workflows that act on a given `folder ID`, and
have a flow with a trigger type of `WORKFLOW_MANUAL_START`.

You application must be authorized to use the `Manage Box Relay` application
scope within the developer console in to use this endpoint.

tools.name: workflows.postWorkflowsIdStart
tools.description:
Initiates a flow with a trigger type of `WORKFLOW_MANUAL_START`.

You application must be authorized to use the `Manage Box Relay` application
scope within the developer console.

tools.name: zipDownloads.getZipDownloadsIdContent
tools.description:
Returns the contents of a `zip` archive in binary format. This URL does not
require any form of authentication and could be used in a user's browser to
download the archive to a user's device.

By default, this URL is only valid for a few seconds from the creation of
the request for this archive. Once a download has started it can not be
stopped and resumed, instead a new request for a zip archive would need to
be created.

The URL of this endpoint should not be considered as fixed. Instead, use
the [Create zip download](e://post_zip_downloads) API to request to create a
`zip` archive, and then follow the `download_url` field in the response to
this endpoint.

tools.name: zipDownloads.getZipDownloadsIdStatus
tools.description:
Returns the download status of a `zip` archive, allowing an application to
inspect the progress of the download as well as the number of items that
might have been skipped.

This endpoint can only be accessed once the download has started.
Subsequently this endpoint is valid for 12 hours from the start of the
download.

The URL of this endpoint should not be considered as fixed. Instead, use
the [Create zip download](e://post_zip_downloads) API to request to create a
`zip` archive, and then follow the `status_url` field in the response to
this endpoint.

tools.name: zipDownloads.postZipDownloads
tools.description:
Creates a request to download multiple files and folders as a single `zip`
archive file. This API does not return the archive but instead performs all
the checks to ensure that the user has access to all the items, and then
returns a `download_url` and a `status_url` that can be used to download the
archive.

The limit for an archive is either the Account's upload limit or
10,000 files, whichever is met first

tools.name: openapi.previewSpec
tools.description: Preview an OpenAPI document before adding it as a source

tools.name: openapi.addSource
tools.description: Add an OpenAPI source and register its operations as tools
